By JEFFREY GOLDBERG

10/04/2001
Dow Jones News Service
(Copyright (c) 2001, Dow Jones & Company, Inc.)

The numbers are staggering. Enron Corp., the Houston energy trading giant, is
the largest energy trader in the world, with a market capitalization of $100
billion. It is also the biggest bankruptcy in U.S. history, with $40 billion in
debts. And it is the largest bankruptcy filing in U.S. history, with $20 billion in
debts.

Enron's stock price has fallen from $100 in January to $20 today. And its debt has soared from
$20 billion to $40 billion.
